title: CJNG Succession Crisis Enters Week 7 — O3 Council Holds, El Jardinero at Large
detail: Day 43 since El Mencho's killing in Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). Juan Carlos Valencia González 'O3,' El Mencho's stepson, maintains reported control of CJNG through a collective council backed by Grupo Élite — but analysts at RUSI and InSight Crime warn of high fragmentation risk. El Jardinero (Audias Flores Silva) remains the primary rival power center, commanding significant plazas in Jalisco, Nayarit, Zacatecas, and Colima with an active $5 million US bounty. Operativo Enjambre has resulted in 64+ arrests since February 22. 2,500 troops remain deployed in Guadalajara for World Cup security preparation. Sinaloa Cartel's Los Chapitos vs. Los Mayos civil war continues as a parallel conflict, creating opportunistic territorial pressure on CJNG-controlled areas.

title: USMCA Renegotiation Uncertainty Deepens — Trump to Renegotiate, Not Just Review
detail: US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick confirmed that the Trump administration intends to renegotiate USMCA rather than simply review it, introducing trade policy uncertainty through at least July 2026. Mexico remains exempt from the general 10% universal tariff applied to most countries, but Mexican goods not qualifying for USMCA origin rules face 25% tariffs. Mexico closed 2025 with a record trade surplus with the US — 20% larger than 2024 — adding political pressure to the trade relationship. The USMCA renegotiation outcome is now tightly linked to Mexico's security cooperation benchmarks on cartel interdiction and fentanyl trafficking.
